title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
summary: Jack Roberts discusses the removal of Claude Fable 5 and argues that users should run language models locally using Ollama to maintain access to capable models independently of cloud service providers. He demonstrates how to set up local inference and build an open-source NotebookLM alternative, along with a model routing system across multiple providers.
writeup: The video responds to the removal of Claude Fable 5 by making the case for running language models locally with Ollama, so access to capable models does not depend on cloud providers. Jack Roberts walks through setting up local inference, building an open-source alternative to NotebookLM, and constructing a model routing system that spans multiple providers. The practical takeaways are concrete: a local inference setup, a self-hosted document tool, and routing logic that distributes requests across providers.
